Representative tCycIF image showing the spatial expression of clinically relevant antibody–drug conjugate (ADC) targets, including B7-H4, 
FOLR1, and TROP2 .B7-H4TME landscape and spatial immune niches 
were proﬁled in 80 chemo-naïve patients with 
HGSC enrolled in the prospective Oncosys-
OVA clinical trial (NCT06117384). 
RCNs stratify clinical subgroups (HRD vs. HRP),
with HRP tumours enriched in B-cell, Stromal-CAF, 
and stroma-resident immune niches, consistent 
with SPACEstat analysis.tCycIF spatial analysis conﬁrms 
heterogeneous expression of ADC 
targets (B7-H4, TROP2, FOLR1) in 
HGSC, with distinct and 
overlapping TME localisation, 
supporting ongoing eﬀorts to 
map target distribution, assess 
ADC-driven TME modulation, and 
inform patient stratiﬁcation, 
eﬃcacy, and safety.Recurrent driver alterations co-localise with characteristic copy number 
alteration (CNA) signature events (G-score peaks) and are broadly 
dispersed across the genome, consistent with the HGSC CNV landscape; 
this suggests TME structures are associated with broader CNA signature 
clusters rather than single focal drivers, enabling patient stratiﬁcation 
detectable by tCycIF.

andscape; 
this suggests TME structures are associated with broader CNA signature 
clusters rather than single focal drivers, enabling patient stratiﬁcation 
detectable by tCycIF.
In contrast, somatic SNVs and germline variants are relatively sparse on 
the oncoplot, indicating a limited contribution compared with the 
dominant CNA-driven signatures that potentially underlie the observed 
TME phenotypes.In HGSC, prognosis is strongly inﬂuenced 
by the spatial organisation of the tumour 
microenvironment (TME); applying the 
ecological “niche” concept through spatial 
omics, we aim to clarify the relationship 
between niche function and physical 
architecture.
We developed an integrative framework 
combining tissue-based cyclic 
immunoﬂuorescence (tCycIF), whole-exome 
sequencing (WES), bulk RNA sequencing, 
homologous recombination deﬁciency 
(HRD) status, and clinical outcomes to 
comprehensively characterise tumour–
